label: Vercel
description: Connect to Vercel to manage and monitor frontend deployments, projects, and environments.

form.title: Connect Vercel
form.subtitle: Paste your Vercel personal access token to give Anton access to your deployments and projects.

methods.label: Personal Access Token
methods.description: Generate a token from Vercel's account settings and paste it here. No OAuth app setup required — works instantly for personal accounts and teams.
methods.how_to:
## Generate a Vercel Personal Access Token

Vercel personal access tokens grant API access scoped to your account and any teams you belong to.

### Steps

1. Log in to [vercel.com](https://vercel.com) and click your avatar in the top-right corner.
2. Go to **Settings** (account-level, not a project's settings).
3. In the left sidebar, click **Tokens**.
4. Click **Create** (top-right of the token list).
5. Give the token a name like `Anton` and choose an expiration (or **No expiration** for a long-lived token).
6. Select the **Scope** — choose **Full Account** or restrict to a specific team as needed.
7. Click **Create Token**. Copy the token shown — it is only displayed once.
8. Paste the token into the **API Token** field below.

### Notes

- The token is shown only once at creation. If you lose it, delete it and create a new one.
- Team-scoped tokens only grant access to that team's projects; use a full-account token if you need cross-team access.
- You can revoke the token at any time from **Settings → Tokens**.

fields.name: api_token
fields.label: API Token
fields.description: Personal access token from Vercel → Settings → Tokens. Shown only once at creation.

fields.name: team_id
fields.label: Team ID (optional)
fields.description: If your projects live under a team, enter the team ID (found in the team's Settings → General). Leave blank to use your personal account.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (BYOK)
methods.description: Use your own Vercel OAuth application to authenticate via browser-based authorization. Requires creating an OAuth app in Vercel's integrations settings.
methods.how_to:
## OAuth 2.0 with a Vercel OAuth App

This method uses a browser-based authorization flow. You must first create an OAuth app in your Vercel account.

### Steps

1. Log in to [vercel.com](https://vercel.com) and open **Settings → Integrations** or visit [vercel.com/account/oauth-apps](https://vercel.com/account/oauth-apps).
2. Click **Create OAuth App**.
3. Fill in the app name (e.g. `Anton`), homepage URL, and set the **Callback URL** to `http://127.0.0.1` (Anton's local redirect).
4. Click **Create**. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** shown.
5. Paste both values into the fields below and click **Submit**.
6. A browser window will open for you to authorize the app on your Vercel account.

### Notes

- OAuth apps are recommended when you need delegated access on behalf of multiple users.
- For personal or single-team use, the **Personal Access Token** method is simpler.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: Client ID from your Vercel OAuth app settings.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Client secret from your Vercel OAuth app settings. Shown only once at creation.
